race.name: California International Marathon
race.description: Point-to-point net-downhill marathon from Folsom Dam to the California State Capitol in Sacramento. One of the fastest marathon courses in the US — the 340 ft net drop doesn't make it easy (the early rollers demand respect) but it does make sub-3 accessible on a good day.

sources.title: CIM Official Course Map — Run SRA
sources.verified_quote: Net elevation drop of 340 feet from start at Folsom Dam (366 ft) to finish at the State Capitol (26 ft)

phases.label: Folsom Dam drop
phases.note: Downhill opening from the dam. Crowds surge here — let them. Settle into goal pace by mile 1. The descent is doing you a favor; don't add to it with a sprint.

phases.label: Orangevale rollers
phases.note: The hilliest section of the course — a series of short, punchy rollers through Orangevale and Fair Oaks. Run these by effort, not pace. The uphills are short; recover on the downs. This is where many runners blow their sub-3 by chasing splits.

phases.label: Fair Oaks hills
phases.note: The rollers continue but begin trending net-downhill through Fair Oaks. If you've been patient, you'll start passing people here. Hold composure — the real payoff starts after mile 10.

phases.label: Auburn Blvd descent
phases.note: The fastest mile-stretch on the course — a noticeable drop through the Auburn corridor. Let gravity assist but don't overcook it. You have 16 miles left. Use it as a reset to recheck form and nutrition.

phases.label: Sacramento valley
phases.note: 15 miles of gentle, sustained downhill to the Capitol. This is CIM's gift — run the math, trust your pace, don't get greedy. Miles 20-22 are where people start to fade; if you've been even-keel, this is where you pass them. The finish chute on Capitol Mall is fast.

landmarks.label: Folsom Dam · 366 ft
landmarks.note: Start. Highest point of the course.

landmarks.label: Auburn Blvd
landmarks.note: End of the rollers. The course flattens and trends consistently downhill from here.

landmarks.label: State Capitol · 26 ft
landmarks.note: Finish. Lowest point of the course.

warnings.early_pace: The opening descent from the dam tricks runners into going out too fast. First 2 miles feel easy — they are not representative of the course.
warnings.rollers: Miles 2-10 require patience. Running by effort (not pace) through the rollers is the key to a negative split on the back half.
warnings.quad_load: Net downhill courses accumulate eccentric quad load. Build specific downhill training (long runs on net-downhill routes) in the 8 weeks before race day.
